title: A10. Riemann Hypothesis Super-Rigidity Gap
label: mx1_transmute_01
task_text:
Solve this Super-Eywa grading-bank question.
Question ID: atlas-derived-A10-riemann-hypothesis-super-rigidity-gap
Title: A10. Riemann Hypothesis Super-Rigidity Gap

Problem:
Can a Hermitian matrix eigenvalue spectrum match the Riemann zeta zeros' 47% super-rigidity (Delta_3_sat = 0.155)?

Grading target:
Binary:

- correct conclusion
- identification of the quantitative gap: 0.155 vs 0.24

Return format:
FINAL_ANSWER: <your answer>
JUSTIFICATION: <brief justification>
root_result_excerpt:
The user wants to simplify a complex mathematical physics question into its core components for another agent to solve.
node_root_helper_01 (transmuted_child): Determine if a Hermitian matrix eigenvalue spectrum can match the super-rigidity of the Riemann zeta zeros, specifically regarding the Delta_3 statistic. Compare the Riemann zeta zeros' value (Delta_3_sat = 0.155) against the standard value
final_result_excerpt:
FINAL_ANSWER: No, gap is 0.085
JUSTIFICATION: Zeta zeros are more rigid than standard Hermitian matrices.

root_orchestration.decision_notes: The user wants to simplify a complex mathematical physics question into its core components for another agent to solve.

grading.expected:
No.

Best construction achieves Delta_3_sat = 0.24.
GUE statistics are easy, but super-rigidity requires prime-orbit structure no matrix encodes.
grading.grading_notes: The user correctly identified the binary answer ('No') and correctly identified the quantitative gap (0.24 - 0.155 = 0.085), satisfying both requirements of the grading target.
